title: Verify auth middleware implementation for route protection
description: Verified: auth middleware and withAuth HOC already exist and are applied to all protected routes. withAuth (src/auth/withAuth.ts:55) extracts Bearer tokens, validates JWTs via AuthService.verifyAccessToken, checks RBAC roles, returns 401 for missing/invalid/expired tokens. All 14 protected API routes already use withAuth with proper role-based access. auth-middleware.test.ts (8 tests) and auth-service.test.ts (16 tests) all pass. Only /api/health (intentionally public) and /api/csrf-token (login flow) lack JWT auth. No code changes needed.
existing_patterns: src/auth/withAuth.ts - HOC wrapper pattern: withAuth(handler, options) wraps async route handlers, extracts Bearer token via extractBearerToken, validates JWT via AuthService.verifyAccessToken, returns 401 for missing/invalid tokens, attaches user to context for downstream handlers

src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts - Factory pattern: createAuthMiddleware(userStore, sessionStore, jwtService) returns async function that does rate limiting + token validation + session lookup + user lookup
src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JWT TokenPayload type with userId, email, role, sessionId, generation, iat, exp; signAccessToken(15min), signRefreshToken(7days), verify() throws on invalid/expired tokens
src/auth/_auth.ts - extractBearerToken helper and checkRole RBAC helper used by withAuth
